mapping.a_element: generalized Kaehler structures derived from Courant algebroids and Poisson manifolds
mapping.b_element: generalized complex structures describing topologically twisted sigma-models and D-branes

mapping.a_element: Poisson varieties and their non-holomorphic isomorphisms
mapping.b_element: noncommutative deformations of Calabi-Yau manifolds and interpolation between A-model and B-model

mapping.a_element: configurations of branes on Poisson varieties
positive Poisson line bundles on holomorphic Poisson manifolds
mapping.b_element: B-field and noncommutativity parameters in the deformed sigma-model

brief_justification: Both papers employ generalized complex/geometric structures (generalized Kaehler and generalized complex structures) as the core mathematical machinery to unify and extend classical geometric frameworks (Poisson manifolds and Calabi-Yau sigma-models). The role of branes in both contexts is governed by these generalized structures, and the deformation parameters (Poisson line bundles/B-field) play analogous roles in bridging holomorphic and non-holomorphic or commutative and noncommutative regimes.
